segments.text: Life is short and full of trouble,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: like a flower that blooms and withers, like a passing shadow that soon disappears.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Do you even notice me, God, and why do you have to drag me to court?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who can bring something clean of what is unclean? No one.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You have determined how long we shall live—the number of months, a time limit on our lives.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So leave us alone and give us some peace—so like a laborer we could enjoy a few hours of rest at the end of the day.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even a tree that's cut down has the hope of sprouting again, of sending up shoots and continuing to live.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even though its roots grow old in the earth, and its stump dies in the ground,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: just a trickle of water will make it bud and grow branches like a young plant.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But human beings die, their strength dwindles away; they perish, and where are they then?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Like water evaporating from a lake and a river that dries up and disappears,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: so human beings lie down and don't get up again. Until the heavens cease to exist they will not awake from their sleep.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I wish you would hide me in Sheol;  conceal me there until your anger is gone. Set a definite time for me there, and remember me!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Will the dead live again? Then I would have hope through all my time of trouble until my release comes.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You would call and I would answer you; you would long for me, the being that you made.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then you would look after me and wouldn't be watching me to see if I sinned.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My sins would be sealed up in a bag and you would cover my guilt.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But just as the mountains crumble and fall, and the rocks tumble down;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: as water wears away the stones, as floods wash away the soil, so you destroy the hope people have.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You continually overpower them and they pass away; you distort their faces in death and send them away.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Their children may become important or fall from their positions, but they don't know or see any of this.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As people die they only know their own pain and are sad for themselves.
segments.speaker: Unknown
